1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
40	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
41	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

9	/-!
10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
28	
29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
36	
37	## Status (iter-202 Lane TS — file-skeleton scaffold)
38	
39	This file is the **iter-202 Lane TS** file-skeleton: each of the 4 pinned
40	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
41	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`) with a
42	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-203+ work: the `tensorObj` definition lifts
43	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through sheafification, and the consumer
44	`PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` then closes the residual `addCommGroup`
45	sorry of `RelPicFunctor.lean` L235.
46	
47	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
48	
49	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (def) — the substrate binary
50	   operation `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`,
51	   lifting `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` on underlying presheaves
52	   composed with sheafification on the small Zariski site.
53	   Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`.
54	
55	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality` (def) — the
56	   functorial action of `⊗` on morphisms: a pair `f : M ⟶ M'`, `g : N ⟶ N'`
57	   determines `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`.
58	   Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`.
59	
60	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory` (instance) — the
61	   monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X` with tensor `⊗`, unit
62	   `O_X`, associator, unitors, and braiding inherited from
63	   `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal` under sheafification.
64	   Per blueprint `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal`.
65	
66	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (def) — the
67	   `AddCommGroup` structure on the relative Picard quotient
68	   `Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)`, built via the
69	   `tensorObj` substrate. This is the iter-204+ closure target for the Lane RPF
70	   L235/L266-269 `addCommGroup` residual sorry.
71	   Per blueprint `thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`.
72	
73	Plus (PUSH-BEYOND) the supporting helper lemmas of the lift section
74	(`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`,
75	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`,

94	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base change along a ring iso commutes
95	with `⊗` (the H2 "bottom gap" of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`)
96	
97	For a *ring isomorphism* `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `A`, `B`, base change along
98	`e` (giving each `S`-module its `R`-module structure via `Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom`)
99	commutes with the tensor product: the canonical map `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b` is an
100	`R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B`. Equivalently, `restrictScalars`
101	along a ring iso is *strong* monoidal — the lax tensorator is invertible. Mathlib
102	has `ModuleCat.extendScalars` strong monoidal but `restrictScalars` only
103	`LaxMonoidal`; this ring-iso strong upgrade is absent and is the documented "REAL
104	bottom gap" (H2) of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
105	
106	section RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
107	
108	open TensorProduct
109	
110	variable {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
111	
112	/-- The `R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B` (`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ b`),
113	where the `R`-module structures are base-changed along the ring iso `e : R ≃+* S`.
114	Base change along a ring iso commutes with `⊗`. -/
115	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
116	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] :
117	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
118	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
119	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
120	    TensorProduct R A B ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B := by
121	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
122	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
123	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
124	  -- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`, an `R`-bilinear-to-linear lift.
125	  let fwd : TensorProduct R A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B :=
126	    TensorProduct.lift
127	      { toFun := fun a =>
128	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
129	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
130	            map_smul' := fun r b => by
131	              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
132	              change a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
133	              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
134	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
135	        map_smul' := fun r a => by
136	          ext b
137	          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
138	            AddHom.coe_mk]
139	          change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
140	          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] }
141	  -- Inverse: `a ⊗ₜ[S] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[R] b`. Built as an additive lift out of the
142	  -- `S`-tensor (scalar-swap compatibility uses `s • a = e.symm s •ᵣ a`), then
143	  -- shown `R`-linear (`R` acting on the `S`-tensor via `e`).
144	  let bwdAdd : TensorProduct S A B →+ TensorProduct R A B :=
145	    TensorProduct.liftAddHom
146	      { toFun := fun a =>
147	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
148	            map_zero' := by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
149	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add] }
150	        map_zero' := by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]
151	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
152	      (fun s a b => by
153	        simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
154	        -- `(s • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = a ⊗ₜ[R] (s • b)`; move the `S`-scalar through `e.symm`.
155	        have hsa : (s • a) = (e.symm s : R) • a := by
156	          change s • a = e (e.symm s) • a; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
157	        have hsb : (s • b) = (e.symm s : R) • b := by
158	          change s • b = e (e.symm s) • b; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
159	        rw [hsa, hsb]; exact TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _ )
160	  let bwd : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct R A B :=
161	    { toFun := bwdAdd
162	      map_add' := bwdAdd.map_add
163	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
164	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
165	        -- `R` acts on the `S`-tensor via `e`; reduce to additive `S`-scalar action.
166	        change bwdAdd (e r • x) = r • bwdAdd x
167	        induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
168	        | zero => simp
169	        | tmul a b =>
170	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
171	            change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = r • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
172	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
173	            rfl
174	        | add x y hx hy =>
175	            rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, hx, hy, smul_add] }
176	  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear fwd bwd ?_ ?_
177	  · -- right inverse `fwd ∘ bwd = id`. The composite is `R`-linear over the
178	    -- `S`-tensor, so check on additive generators by induction.
179	    refine LinearMap.ext fun x => ?_
180	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
181	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
182	    | zero => simp
183	    | tmul a b =>
184	        change fwd (bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
185	        change fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
186	        simp only [fwd, TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
187	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add bwd, map_add fwd, hx, hy]
188	  · -- left inverse `bwd ∘ fwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[R] b` (composite `R`-linear over the
189	    -- `R`-tensor, so `TensorProduct.ext'` applies).
190	    refine TensorProduct.ext' fun a b => ?_
191	    change bwdAdd (fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
192	    change bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
193	    rfl
194	
195	/-- The forward map of `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` on a simple tensor:
196	`a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`. -/
197	@[simp] lemma restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul (e : R ≃+* S)
198	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] (a : A) (b : B) :
199	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
200	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
201	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
202	    restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e A B (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b := by
203	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
204	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
205	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
206	  simp only [restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv, LinearEquiv.ofLinear_apply,
207	    TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
208	
209	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is strong monoidal: the
210	lax tensorator `μ` is an isomorphism.** This is the "REAL bottom gap" (H2) of
211	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. For a ring iso `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `M₁, M₂`,
212	the underlying map of the lax tensorator
213	`μ : restrictScalars(M₁) ⊗_R restrictScalars(M₂) ⟶ restrictScalars(M₁ ⊗_S M₂)`
214	sends `m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ ↦ m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul`), which is exactly
215	the forward `R`-linear equivalence `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e`, hence is
216	bijective, hence an isomorphism. Mathlib has `extendScalars` strong monoidal but
217	only `restrictScalars` lax; this ring-iso strong upgrade is the documented absent
218	ingredient. -/
219	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ (e : R ≃+* S) (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
220	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) := by
221	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
222	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂)
223	      = ⇑(restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂) := by
224	    ext z
225	    induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
226	    | zero => rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm
227	    | tmul a b =>
228	        erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul]
229	        exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul e M₁ M₂ a b).symm
230	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add, hx, hy]; exact (map_add _ x y).symm
231	  rw [hfun]
232	  exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂).bijective
233	
234	/-- **The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is an
235	isomorphism.** Its underlying map is the ring map `e` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`),
236	which is bijective since `e` is a ring equivalence. -/
237	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε (e : R ≃+* S) :
238	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) := by
239	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
240	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) = ⇑e := by
241	    ext r
242	    exact ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η (f := e.toRingHom) r
243	  rw [hfun]
244	  exact e.bijective
245	
246	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is (strong) monoidal.**
247	The packaged `Functor.Monoidal` structure obtained from the lax structure by
248	inverting `ε` (`restrictScalars_isIso_ε`) and `μ` (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ`). This
249	is the clean, reusable strong-monoidal upgrade that Mathlib provides for
250	`extendScalars` but not `restrictScalars`; it is the ModuleCat-level core of the H2
251	ingredient of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
252	@[implicit_reducible]
253	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv (e : R ≃+* S) :
254	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom).Monoidal := by
255	  haveI : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) :=
256	    restrictScalars_isIso_ε e
257	  haveI : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) :=
258	    fun M₁ M₂ => restrictScalars_isIso_μ e M₁ M₂
259	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
260	
261	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal tensorator.** For an arbitrary
262	*bijective* ring hom `f : R →+* S`, the lax tensorator of `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f`
263	is an isomorphism. This is the form consumed by the presheaf-level lift, where the
264	componentwise ring map `(α.app X).hom` of a `NatIso` of ring presheaves is bijective
265	but not literally presented as `(_ : R ≃+* S).toRingHom`. -/
266	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f)
267	    (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
268	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f) M₁ M₂) := by
269	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
270	  rw [hfe]
271	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_μ (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf) M₁ M₂
272	
273	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal unit.** Companion of
274	`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective` for the lax unit `ε`. -/
275	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f) :
276	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f)) := by
277	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
278	  rw [hfe]
279	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_ε (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf)
280	
281	end RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
282	
283	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
284	
285	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
286	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
287	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
288	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
289	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
290	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
291	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
292	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
293	
294	namespace PresheafOfModules
295	
296	universe v'
297	
298	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
299	
300	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
301	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
302	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
303	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
304	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
305	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
306	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
307	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
308	  naturality {X Y} f := by
309	    ext r
310	    dsimp
311	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
312	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
313	    simp only [map_one]
314	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
315	
316	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
317	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
318	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
319	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
320	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
321	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
322	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
323	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
324	  app X := by
325	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
326	  naturality {X Y} f := by
327	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
328	    dsimp
329	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
330	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
331	    rfl
332	
333	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
334	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
335	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
336	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
337	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
338	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
339	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
340	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
341	  μ_natural_left := by
342	    intro X Y f X'
343	    ext1 Z
344	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
345	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
346	  μ_natural_right := by
347	    intro X Y X' f
348	    ext1 Z
349	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
350	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
351	  associativity := by
352	    intro M N P
353	    ext1 Z
354	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
355	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
356	  left_unitality := by
357	    intro M
358	    ext1 Z
359	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
360	      (M.obj Z)
361	  right_unitality := by
362	    intro M
363	    ext1 Z
364	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
365	      (M.obj Z)
366	
367	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
368	sheafification localizer
369	
370	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
371	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
372	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
373	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
374	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
375	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
376	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
377	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
378	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
379	structure. -/
380	
381	section FlatWhisker
382	
383	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
384	
385	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
386	
387	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
388	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
389	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
390	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
391	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
392	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
393	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
394	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
395	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
396	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
397	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
398	
399	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
400	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
401	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
402	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
403	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
404	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
405	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
406	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
407	
408	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
409	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
410	half. -/
411	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
412	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
413	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
414	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
415	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
416	  constructor
417	  intro U s
418	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
419	  | zero =>
420	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
421	      intro V i _
422	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
423	  | tmul a b =>
424	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
425	      intro V i hi
426	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
427	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
428	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
429	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
430	      congr 1
431	  | add s t hs ht =>
432	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
433	      intro V i hi
434	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
435	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
436	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
437	
438	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
439	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
440	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
441	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
442	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
443	injectivity half. -/
444	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
445	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
446	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
447	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
448	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
449	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
450	  constructor
451	  intro X ξ η h
452	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
453	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
454	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
455	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
456	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
457	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
458	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
459	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
460	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
461	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
462	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
463	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
464	  have hex : Function.Exact
465	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
466	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
467	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
468	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
469	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
470	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
471	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
472	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
473	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
474	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
475	    intro ζ
476	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
477	    | zero =>
478	        rw [map_zero]
479	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
480	    | tmul a kk =>
481	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
482	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
483	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
484	          rw [map_zero]
485	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
486	          exact kk.2
487	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
488	        intro V f hf
489	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
490	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
491	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
492	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
493	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
494	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
495	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
496	        rw [map_add]
497	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
498	        intro V f hf
499	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
500	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
501	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
502	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
503	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
504	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
505	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
506	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
507	  intro V f hf
508	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
509	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
510	  exact hf
511	
512	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
513	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
514	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
515	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
516	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
517	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
518	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
519	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
520	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
521	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
522	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
523	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
524	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
525	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
526	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
527	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
528	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
529	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
530	
531	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
532	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
533	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
534	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
535	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
536	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
537	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
538	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
539	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
540	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
541	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
542	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
543	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
544	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
545	  have hconj : g ▷ F
546	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
547	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
548	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
549	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
550	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
551	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
552	  exact hwl
553	
554	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
555	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
556	
557	The flat whiskering above (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat`) needs the SECTIONWISE
558	flatness instance `∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible
559	sheaves over non-affine opens and is therefore OFF the associator critical path
560	(iter-212 finding). The associator only ever whiskers the sheafification UNIT
561	`η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`), not merely locally
562	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves
563	local bijectivity with NO flatness hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x}
564	⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso on
565	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
566	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*
567	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. This is exactly the
568	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points
569	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, route (d)). -/
570	
571	section WhiskerOfW
572	
573	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
574	
575	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
576	
577	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity
578	(flatness-free).** For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g`
579	whose underlying additive-presheaf map is locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the
580	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective.
581	
582	This is the single residual ingredient of the associator
583	`tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). The mathematics is the stalkwise computation
584	`(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: a `J.W`-morphism on the topological site of `X`
585	is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + the conservative-family
586	characterisation `hP.W_iff`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and
587	tensoring an isomorphism `g_x` by `id_{F_x}` yields an isomorphism, so `F ◁ g` is a
588	stalkwise iso, hence locally bijective, hence locally injective — for *any* `F`,
589	needing NEITHER sectionwise flatness NOR local triviality of `F`.
590	
591	The two Mathlib-side ingredients this stalkwise argument needs are
592	(d.1) the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level `J.W` on `Opens X` and
593	(d.2) the commutation `(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` of the stalk with the
594	presheaf-of-modules tensor (stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight`
595	preserve filtered colimits over a module category). Neither ships at the
596	`PresheafOfModules` level at the pinned commit; porting them is the genuine residual
597	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, Decision 3, porting ingredients d.1/d.2). Stated
598	here with its substantive intended type so the rest of the associator closes against
599	it; see the task result for the precise missing Mathlib statements. -/
600	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
601	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
602	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
603	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
604	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
605	  -- ROUTE (e) residual: `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` is an iso since `g_x` is
606	  -- (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso); tensoring by `id` of an iso is flatness-free.
607	  --
608	  -- iter-214 CORRECTION (Step 0 + d.1 partial): the module-level stalk is NOT
609	  -- Mathlib-absent — `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk` supplies, for
610	  -- `X : TopCat`, `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module
611	  -- `Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` and `germ_smul`. The
612	  -- linear-stalk-map packaging (ingredient d.1) is now built project-side:
613	  -- `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap` (+ `stalkLinearMap_germ`,
614	  -- `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`), all axiom-clean.
615	  --
616	  -- The TWO residual gaps to close this sorry (both require SPECIALISING this lemma
617	  -- to the topological site `C = Opens X` — the general-site statement here has no
618	  -- stalks; decl is UNPROTECTED so the specialisation is free, and the only consumer
619	  -- `tensorObj_assoc_iso` already works over `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`):
620	  --   (d.1-bridge) `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W (toPresheaf-image) ↔ ∀ x,
621	  --     IsIso (stalkFunctor Ab x map)` — assemble from `HasEnoughPoints
622	  --     (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)` (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Points.lean`) +
623	  --     `W_iff`, OR from `WEqualsLocallyBijective` + the TopCat criteria
624	  --     `locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks` /
625	  --     `app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`
626	  --     (needs `presheafFiber ≅ TopCat.Presheaf.stalk`, the Mathlib TODO bridge).
627	  --   (d.2) stalk ⊗ commutation `(F ⊗ᵖ M)_x ≅ F_x ⊗_{R_x} M_x` naturally identifying
628	  --     `(F ◁ g)_x` with `LinearMap.lTensor F_x (stalkLinearMap g x)` — "tensor
629	  --     commutes with the filtered colimit defining the stalk" over the varying ring;
630	  --     genuinely Mathlib-absent (largest piece). Then `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`
631	  --     + `LinearEquiv.lTensor` finish (flatness-free). See task result for full decomp.
632	  sorry
633	
634	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
635	ROUTE (d) replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a
636	locally bijective `g` (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`.
637	Local surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness);
638	local injectivity is the flatness-free stalkwise residual
639	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. No flatness, no local triviality. -/
640	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
641	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
642	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
643	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
644	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
645	  have hbij := hg
646	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
647	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
648	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
649	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
650	
651	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
652	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
653	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
654	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
655	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
656	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
657	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
658	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
659	  have hconj : g ▷ F
660	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
661	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
662	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
663	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
664	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
665	  exact hwl
666	
667	end WhiskerOfW
668	
669	/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
670	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
671	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
672	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
673	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
674	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
675	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
676	read at a single morphism. -/
677	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
678	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
679	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
680	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
681	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
682	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
683	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
684	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
685	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
686	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
687	  exact h2
688	
689	end FlatWhisker

691	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
692	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
693	
694	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
695	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
696	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
697	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
698	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
699	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
700	
701	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
702	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
703	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
704	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
705	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
706	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
707	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
708	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
709	
710	section StalkLinearMap
711	
712	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
713	
714	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
715	
716	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
717	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
718	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
719	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
720	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
721	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
722	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
723	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
724	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
725	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
726	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
727	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
728	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
729	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
730	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
731	  map_smul' r s := by
732	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
733	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
734	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
735	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
736	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
737	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
738	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
739	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
740	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
741	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
742	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
743	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
744	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
745	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
746	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
747	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
748	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
749	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
750	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
751	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
752	      intro z
753	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
754	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
755	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
756	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
757	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
758	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
759	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
760	
761	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
762	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
763	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
764	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
765	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
766	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
767	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
768	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
769	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
770	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
771	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
772	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
773	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
774	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
775	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
776	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
777	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
778	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
779	
780	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
781	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
782	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
783	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
784	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
785	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
786	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
787	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
788	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
789	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
790	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
791	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
792	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
793	
794	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
795	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
796	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
797	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
798	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
799	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
800	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
801	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
802	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
803	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
804	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
805	
806	end StalkLinearMap
807	
808	end PresheafOfModules
809	
810	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the presheaf-level pushforward adjunction (H1)
811	
812	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
813	pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`
814	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`, L154/L73/L226) to the
815	`PresheafOfModules` level. Every line of the sheaf template already manipulates
816	`.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf`
817	wrapper and the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of
818	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair `adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction
819	`pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against the existing
820	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
821	the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
822	restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/
823	
824	namespace PresheafOfModules
825	
826	open CategoryTheory Functor
827	
828	section PushforwardNatTrans
829	
830	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
831	
832	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
833	  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
834	  (φ : S ⟶ G.op ⋙ R)
835	
836	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
837	`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
838	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
839	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
840	noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
841	    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
842	  app M :=
843	    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
844	      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
845	        ext x
846	        dsimp
847	        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
848	          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
849	        simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
850	  naturality := fun {M N} f => by
851	    ext U x
852	    exact congr($(f.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
853	
854	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R)
855	    (U : Cᵒᵖ) (x) :
856	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).app U x = M.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
857	
858	end PushforwardNatTrans
859	
860	section PushforwardCongr
861	
862	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
863	
864	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
865	  {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
866	
867	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardCongr`.** Pushforwards along equal morphisms of presheaves of
868	rings are isomorphic. De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`
869	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:73`), dropping the `fullyFaithfulForget` preimage (the
870	presheaf-level `pushforward` lands in `PresheafOfModules` directly). -/
871	noncomputable def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ) :
872	    pushforward.{u} φ ≅ pushforward.{u} ψ :=
873	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun M ↦
874	    PresheafOfModules.isoMk
875	      (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (R := S.obj U) (S := R.obj _)
876	        (f := (φ.app U).hom) (g := (ψ.app U).hom) (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
877	      (fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) (fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)
878	
879	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
880	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
881	    ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
882	
883	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
884	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
885	    ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
886	
887	end PushforwardCongr
888	
889	section PushforwardAdj
890	
891	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
892	
893	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
894	  {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
895	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
896	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
897	  (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S)
898	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R = ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ)
899	  (H₂ : φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S = 𝟙 S)
900	
901	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

903	pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. De-sheafification of
904	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), dropping the

907	`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
908	noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
